title: Trapdoor Bridge
description: A retractable floor of trapdoors that opens or closes on a redstone signal, creating a hidden pit or a walkable bridge. Iron trapdoors are controlled by redstone; when powered they open (horizontal), unpowered they close (vertical, solid). A lever or T flip-flop toggles the bridge.

steps.title: Foundation frame
steps.explanation: Place stone blocks on both ends as anchor frames: blocks at x=0 and x=3 at y=0. These are the wall-mounts the trapdoors hinge on.

steps.title: Iron trapdoors
steps.explanation: Place iron trapdoors at (1,0,0) and (2,0,0), both hinged on the bottom edge (face=bottom). When unpowered they lie flat (open=false, players can walk across). When powered they fold up (open=true, revealing a gap). Iron trapdoors respond to redstone; wooden ones need a direct power source.

steps.title: Control lever and wiring
steps.explanation: Lever at (0,1,0) on the anchor stone. Dust at (1,1,0) carries signal to the east trapdoor at x=1. A second dust or repeater at (2,1,0) carries signal to the west trapdoor at x=2. Both trapdoors open simultaneously when lever is flipped — the bridge retracts.
